original: Third Council of Constantinople (6th ecumenical council) opens
East France and West France recognize each other in the Treaty of Bonn
Ensisheim Meteorite strikes a wheat field near the village of Ensisheim in Alsace, France. Oldest meteorite with a known date of impact.
Pierre Gassendi observes 1st ever transit of Mercury predicted by Kepler
Puritan reformist preacher Anne Hutchinson is tried in Massachusetts Bay Colony as a heretic, found guilty and banished
The Treaty of the Pyrenees signed by representatives of French KingLouis XIV& Spanish king Philip IV
1st edition of "London Gazette" is published as "The Oxford Gazette"
France & Spain sign the Treaty of the Escorial (the first 'Pacte de Famille' between the Bourbon kings of France and Spain)
Organgists in the Netherlands revolt under Daniel Raap
Lord Dunmore promises freedom to male slaves who join British army
The oldest performing musical organization in the United States is founded in Stoughton, Massachusetts, as the Stoughton Musical Society
French troops conquer Nijmegen
It becomes illegal for women in Paris to wear trousers without a Police permit (annulled 2013)
First sitting of the States-General in The Hague
St Petersburg floods to 4.20m, city's greatest flood
State of Gran Colombia disbands
In Alton, Illinois, abolitionist printer Elijah P. Lovejoy shot dead (age 34) by pro-slavery mob while attempting to protect his printing shop from being destroyed a third time.
Battle of Port Royal, fought in Port Royal Sound, South Carolina ends, Union forces capture Fort Walker and Fort Beauregard (US Civil War)
Battles of Rappahannock Station and Kelly's Ford, Virginia
Second session of the Congress of the Confederate States of America reconvenes
Cargo ship Mary Celeste sails from Staten Island for Genoa, mysteriously found abandoned four weeks later
Verney Cameron is the first European to cross equatorial Africa
Edward Bouchet is the first African American to receive a Ph.D. from a US college (Yale)
Meharry Medical College forms at Central Tennessee College
Canadian Pacific Railway completed at Craigellachie - connects British Columbia with Eastern Canada
US State Colorado accepts female suffrage
Battle of Leliefontein: British-Canadian forces repel a Boer force in Transvaal and the Royal Canadian Dragoons win three Victoria Crosses
A French fleet seizes the customs house on the Turkish-ruled island of Mytilene after Turks refuse to settle France's indemnity claims for losses suffered by French subjects in 1896
Dynamite explodes on locomotive kills engineer Jesus Garcia in Mexico
Test tokens are struck in the first production of Canadian coins
Dutch capture Venezuelan navy
Knights of St Peter Claver founded in Mobile, Alabama; Ladies Auxiliary formed in 1922
First air freight shipment is undertaken by the Wright Brothers and department store owner Max Morehouse from Dayton, Ohio, to Columbus, Ohio
Great Lakes Storm the "White Hurricane" begins with 90 mph winds and 35 foot waves, (lasts till 10 Nov), will sink 19 ships and strand 19 more, killing over 250 people
First issue of The New Republic magazine is published
German Chinese concession of Kiaochow Bay and its center at Tsingtao are captured by Japanese forces
Japanese attack German concession on Chinese peninsula of Shanghai
Grand Duke Nikolai Nikolayevich warns Tsar of uprising
British capture Gaza, Palestine, from Turks
Kurt Eisner overthrows the Wittelsbach dynasty in the Kingdom of Bavaria as a revolutionary uprising spreads throughout Germany
US police raid offices of Union of Russian Workers
Ed Wynn's musical revue "The Perfect Fool" premieres in NYC
Hungary passes a law invalidating the Habsburg line of succession to the throne
Italians liberal-national party joins fascist
First broadcast of "Buck Rogers in the 25th Century" on CBS Radio
Pennsylvania voters overturn blue law, by permitting Sunday sports
Arthur L. Mitchell, becomes first African American Democratic US congressman (Illinois)
Battle of Madrid begins

Polish Jewish teenager Herschel Grynszpan shoots Nazi German diplomat Ernst vom Rath in Paris, to avenge the German expulsion of Jews from Poland; vom Rath dies two days later, sparking the Kristallnacht  violence
Stravinsky's Symfonie in C premieres in Chicago
Tacoma Narrows (Galloping Gertie) Bridge collapses in Washington
British air attacks on Berlin, Mannheim & Ruhrgebied
Last scoreless tie in the NFL, Detroit Lions 0, NY Giants 0
Franklin D. Rooseveltis re-elected President of the United States for a record fourth term, defeating Republican candidate Thomas E. Dewey
Train crashes in tunnel of Aguadilla Spain; about 500 die
King Faruk disbands Egyptian parliament
Carlo Terron's "Processo Agli Innocenti" premieres in Milan
French women and children leave the Hanoi/Tonkin Delta
Constitution of Jordan passes
WIS TV channel 10 in Columbia, SC (NBC) begins broadcasting
Cleveland Browns' Chet Hanulak sets club record with 7 punt returns & win by their largest margin of victory (59) beating Wash 62-3
US spy plane shot down North of Japan
Supreme Court of Baltimore bans segregation in public recreational areas
Phillies pitcher Jack Sanford wins NL Rookie of Year
The Gaither Report (Deterrence & Survival in the Nuclear Age) calls for more American missiles and fallout shelters
WEEQ (now WWTO) TV channel 35 in La Salle, IL (IND) broadcasts for the first time
KNRR TV channel 12 in Pembina, ND (IND) begins broadcasting
France performs underground nuclear test at Ecker Algeria
Eleven German miners are rescued from a collapsed mine after 14 days in the Wunder von Lengede (Miracle of Lengede)
NL keeps Braves in Milwaukee in 1965, may move to Atlanta in 1966
Jean-Claude van Itallie's "America Hurrah" premieres in NYC
Lunar Orbiter 2 launched by US
Carl Stokes elected first African American mayor of a major US city - Cleveland, Ohio
MLB St Louis Cardinals infielder Orlando Cepeda is 1st unanimous NL MVP
Richard G Hatcher elected 1st African-American mayor of Gary, Indiana, served 1968-88
Surveyor 6 launched for soft landing on Moon
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
John Lennon andYoko Onorelease their second album "Wedding Album" in UK
Race riots in Daytona Beach Florida
Incumbent PresidentRichard Nixonis re-elected, defeating Democrat candidate George McGovern in a landslide by winning 49 states
New Jersey becomes the first state to allow girls into Little League
US and Egypt announce the restoration of full diplomatic links
Kidnapped AKZO director Herrema freed in Ireland
Boston Red Sox Jim Rice wins AL MVP
CDA-chairman W Aantjes resigns due to his war past
Marion Barry Jr. elected as Washington, D.C.'s first African American mayor
MLB Chicago Cubs reliever Bruce Sutter wins NL Cy Young Award
France performs nuclear test
Turkey adopts constitution
Ali Haji-Sheikh kicks his 2nd NY Giant record 56 yard field goal
Bomb explodes in US Capitol, causing heavy damage but no injuries
STS 51-A launch scrubbed because of high shear winds
Colombian troops end 27-hr siege of Bogota's Palace of Justice
Tunisian premier Zine al-Abidine fires President Habib Bourguiba
MLB all stars beats Japan 16-8 (Game 3 of 7)
Baltimore Orioles Gregg Olson is 1st relief pitcher to win AL Rookie of Year
Cleveland Indian Sandy Alomar Jr wins AL Rookie of Year, unanimously
Mary Robinson is elected the first female president of Ireland
"Park Your Car in Harvard Yard" opens at Music Box NYC
First NBA game in Delta City: Utah Jazz beat Seattle 103-95
First NBA game at America West Arena, Phoenix Suns beat Clippers 111-105
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania on WXDX 105.9 FM
"3 Sisters" opens at Lunt-Fontanne Theater NYC
Ex-NFL running back and Heisman Trophy winner Mike Rozier, is shot several times in his hometown of Camden, New Jersey; recovers
Wasim Jaffer gets 314* in 2nd cricket FC game for Mumbai v Saurashtra
Two surgeons who performed plastic surgery onAmado Carrillo Fuentesare found dead, encased in concrete drums showing signs of torture
US Drug Enforcement Administration discovers one of the country's largest LSD labs inside a converted military missile silo in Wamego, Kansas
Supersonic commercial aircraft Concorde resumes flying after a 15-month hiatus
Iran bans advertising of products from the United States
The interim government of Iraq calls for a 60-day "state of emergency" as US forces storm the insurgent stronghold of Fallujah during the Iraq War
Joe Liebermanwins re-election to the U.S. Senate as an independent candidate
Jokela school shooting in Tuusula, Finland, results in the deaths of nine people
48 people are killed by a magnitude 7.3 earthquake in Guatemala
Voters in Maine, Maryland and Washington approve measures for same-sex marriage
Sierra Leone is declared free of Ebola by the World Health Organization (death toll 4,000)
Danica Roem becomes the first US transgender person to be elected to a state legislature in the US in North Virginia
Extreme smog in Delhi, India, leads Indian Medical Association to declare "a state of medical emergency"
Melbourne Cup: Joseph O’Brien at 24 is the youngest trainer to win with Rekindling jockeyed by Corey Brown
Opera soprano Audrey Luna sings highest-ever note at Met Opera in New York, A above high C
South Korean golfer Park Sung-hyun becomes the first LPGA rookie to be ranked no. 1
US Democrats dominate off-year elections; Ralph Northam wins Governor of Virginia, Philip Murphy Governor of New Jersey
Ex-marine opens fire at a bar in Thousand Oaks, California, shooting 12, including a police officer, and himself
World's oldest figurative painting of a beast at least 40,000 years old identified in Lubang Jeriji Saléh cave, Indonesian Borneo
Congolese rebel leader Bosco Ntaganda, the "Terminator" is the to be convicted of sexual slavery by the International Criminal Court and is sentenced to 30 years in prison on 18 charges
Humans 1st walked upright 12 million years ago (not 6 million as previously thought), according to study a new species of ape found in Bavaria, Germany, published in "Nature"
Song lyrics have gotten sadder says researchers who studied 50 years of lyrics at University of Exeter, published in journal "Evolutionary Human Sciences"
Former Vice PresidentJoe Bidenis declared the winner of the US presidential race four days after the US election, defeating sitting President Donald Trump
Attempt to assassinate Iraqi Prime Minister Mustafa al-Kadhimi through drone attack on his home in Baghdad
Chinese taikonaut Wang Yaping is the first Chinese female spacewalker, completing a 6 hour space walk as part of the Shenzhou 13 mission
Kyle Larson holds off Martin Truex Jr in the NASCAR Cup Series Championship Race at Phoenix Raceway to earn his 10th win of the season and claim his first Cup Series championship
French tennis star Caroline Garcia scores the biggest title win of her career beating Aryna Sabalenka 7–6, 6–4 in the WTA Finals in Fort Worth, Texas

Australian cricket all-rounder Glenn Maxwell hits 201 not out to guide his team to a 3 wicket World Cup win over Afghanistan in Mumbai; first to score double-ton in a ODI run chase; first non-opener to 200
Cherelle Parker (51) is elected 100th Mayor of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania - first woman and first Black woman mayor
Israeli forces cut off north Gaza, severing it from the southern part of the territory as Hamas  Health Authority says Palestinian death toll is now over 10,000
Ohio votes in two referendums to protect abortion rights under its constitution, and legalize the use of marijuana
Bluey’s World officially opens in Brisbane, Australia, an immersive experience based on the animated children's TV show "Bluey"
First named reference to royal trumpeter John Blanke, the first black person whose image survives from Tudor England, in payment for services byHenry VII
House of Medici dischargesNiccolò Machiavellifrom Florence
French KingHenry IIoccupies Calais
KingLouis XIVof France (13) declared of full age
Richard Steele's sentimental comedy play "The Conscious Lovers" premieres in London
EmpressElizabeth of Russiaproclaims her nephew Peter of Holstein-Gottorp (laterPeter III) her heir
Lewis and ClarkExpedition first sights the Pacific Ocean at the mouth of the Columbia River
Battle of Tippecanoe: GeneralWilliam Henry Harrisondefeats the Native Americans of the Tecumesh Confederation
James Monroere-elected US president
GeneralZachary Taylorelected as 12th President of US
Battle of Belmont, fought in Mississippi County, Missouri begins, first combat test for Brigadier GeneralUlysses S. Grant, Union victory (US Civil War)
Alexander Mackenziebecomes the second Prime Minister of Canada, succeedingJohn A. Macdonald
First cartoon depicting an elephant as the Republican Party symbol is published byThomas Nast
PresidentRutherford B. Hayesand challenger Samuel J. Tilden claim presidential victory as Tilden (D) wins popular vote but Electoral College votes are disputed with Hayes (R)
Canada's Liberal Government led byWilfrid Laurierretains its majority
The Deutsche Opernhaus (now Deutsche Oper Berlin) opens in the Berlin neighborhood of Charlottenburg, with a production ofBeethoven's Fidelio
Amidst the Mexican Revolution and World War I,Woodrow Wilsonis re-elected as President of the United States, defeating Republican candidate Charles E. Hughes
Jeannette Rankin(Rep-R-Montana) is elected to Congress as its first woman Representative
The Russian Soviet Federative Socialist Republic is proclaimed; first Council of People's Commissars is formed withVladimir Leninas leader and includingLeon TrotskyandJoseph Stalin
Robert Goddarddemonstrates tube-launched solid propellant rockets
Soviet dramatic play "Mystery-Bouffe" byVladimir Mayakovskypremieres in Petrograd
Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) opens in the Hecksher Building in New York - its first exhibition featuresCezanne, Gauguin,Seuratandvan Gogh
Chinese Soviet Republic, covering parts of 18 provinces and 4 counties under Communist Party control, is proclaimed by leadersMao Zedongand Zhu De
Fiorello La Guardiais elected the 99th Mayor of New York City
Addressing a meeting of Stakhanovites,Joseph Stalinutters his famous phrase "life has become better"
1st US President to broadcast in a foreign language -FDR, in French
Italian cyclistFausto Coppiestablishes world hour record 45.798km in Vigorelli, Milan, Italy

Columbia Records completes a three-night live recording ofBarbra Streisandat the Bon Soir nightclub in Greenwich Village, NYC, for her debut album; technical issues force them to shelve the release and bring her to a studio to record
Glenn Hallset NHL record of 503 consecutive games as goalie
Richard Nixontells press he won't be available to kick around any more after losing election for Governor of California
New York Yankee catcherElston Howardis first African-American to be voted AL MVP
John LennonmeetsYoko Onoat an avante-garde art exposition at Indica Gallery in London
LBJsigns a bill establishing Corporation for Public Broadcasting
Argentine boxer Carlos Monzon upsets defending championNino Benvenutiof Italy in 12th round KO in Rome to win WBC, WBA middleweight titles
AttorneyJoe Bidenis elected to the US Senate, representing the state of Delaware after defeating longtime incumbent J. Caleb Boggs by just over 3,000 votes;Bidenwould win re-election 6 times

"Gone With The Wind", 1939 Oscar-winning film epic starringVivien LeighandClark Gable, makes broadcast television debut on NBC; aired in two parts over consecutive nights; earns a then record 65% share of TV viewers
Sugar Ray LeonardKO's Donnie LaLonde
NYC elects it's 1st African American mayor (David Dinkins) & 1st female comptroller (Elizabeth Holtzman)
Magic Johnsonannounces he has HIV and retires from the LA Lakers

French Williams driverAlain Prostfinishes 2nd in season ending Australian Grand Prix in Adelaide to claim his 4th F1 World Drivers Championship by 26 points fromAyrton Senna; Prost announces his retirement from F1 racing
"Bean" film directed by Mel Smith and starringRowan Atkinsonas Mr. Bean opens in the US
Dale Jarrettwins the NASCAR Winston Cup Series championship
"TP-2.com" 4th studio album byR. Kellyis released (American Music Awards Favorite Male Soul/R&B Artist, 2001; Billboard Album of the Year, 2001)
Controversial US presidential election betweenGeorge W. BushandAl Goreis inconclusive; the result in Bush's favor is eventually decided by the Supreme Court
Hillary Clintonis elected to the US Senate, becoming first US First Lady to win public office and while still the First Lady
Joe Liebermanis elected to a third Senate term, defeating Republican candidate Philip Giordano
Margaret Atwoodwins the Booker Prize for her novel "The Blind Assassin"
Tim Russertfamously declares the outcome of the 2000 Presidential election will depend on "Florida, Florida, Florida" during NBC coverage
Jerry Sloanbecomes the first coach in NBA history with 1,000 wins for one team after defeating the Oklahoma City Thunder
ActressEmma Thompsonis made a Dame of the British Empire byPrince Williamat Buckingham Palace, London
US PresidentDonald Trumpfires Attorney GeneralJeff Sessionsand appoints Matthew Whitaker in his place as acting Attorney General
Queen Elizabeth IIconfirms she is no longer buying clothes made with real fur
35th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees: Depeche Mode; The Doobie Brothers;Whitney Houston; Nine Inch Nails;The Notorious B.I.G.; T-Rex; Jon Landau; and Irving Azoff
Kamala Harrismakes US history as the 1st woman and 1st woman of color to be elected to the vice presidency (date election called)
Rudy Giulianiholds infamous Trump Campaign press conference at Four Seasons Total Landscaping in Philadelphia to contest the US election results
Australia's Prime MinisterAnthony Albanesemeets Chinese Premier Li Qiang in Beijing, re-establishing dialogue between the countries two leaders after a seven-year gap
Delta Sigma Pi, a professional fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities is founded at New York University.
October Revolution in Russia;Leninand the Bolsheviks seize power, capture the Winter Palace and overthrow the Provisional Government [OS Oct 25]
The 1918 influenza epidemic spreads to Western Samoa, killing 7,542 (about 20% of the population) by the end of the year
United Press erroneously reports WWI armistice had been signed
